spec.overview: This program appears to be a batch processing program that interacts with a card file. It handles opening, reading next records, and closing this file. It also includes error handling for abnormal program termination.
responsibilities: Open and close the card file.
Read records sequentially from the card file.
Handle abnormal program termination and display I/O status.

paragraphs.name: 1000-CARDFILE-GET-NEXT
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is responsible for reading the next record from the card file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to read the next record from a file, handling end-of-file conditions.

paragraphs.name: 0000-CARDFILE-OPEN
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph handles the opening of the card file at the beginning of the program's execution.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement file opening logic, including error checking.

paragraphs.name: 9000-CARDFILE-CLOSE
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is responsible for closing the card file when the program is terminating.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement file closing logic, including error checking.

paragraphs.name: 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is called to initiate an abnormal termination of the program, likely in response to an error.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement program abend logic, potentially by calling a system utility like CEE3ABD.

paragraphs.name: 9910-DISPLAY-IO-STATUS
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is used to display the status of I/O operations, likely for debugging or error reporting.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement logic to display I/O status codes or messages.

migration_risks: The specific file being accessed by 'CARDFILE' is not defined in the artifacts, requiring external investigation.
The exact purpose and usage of the 'CVACT02Y' copybook are not detailed, potentially impacting data structure translation.
The logic within the paragraphs is minimal in the provided artifacts; the actual business logic and error handling might be more complex.
The interaction with the 'CEE3ABD' program for abend handling needs to be mapped to appropriate Java exception handling or logging mechanisms.
